narrative.headline: Top owner complaints for the 2020 Honda Accord
narrative.summary: Owners filed 330 complaints with NHTSA for the 2020 Honda Accord. The single most common topic is the forward collision avoidance system, named in 92 reports (28%). Engine, service brakes, electrical system, and fuel/propulsion round out the top five. A few reports describe a crash, injuries, or one fire; the details for each area are below.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 92 complaints (28% of all reports). 2 mention a crash and 2 mention an injury; no fires or deaths reported.

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2020 HONDA ACCORD. THE CONTACT STATED THAT WHILE DRIVING APPROXIMATELY 50-70 MPH IN THE RAIN, THE VEHICLE LOSS MOTIVE POWER AND STALLED. THE VEHICLE WAS RESTARTED. THE CONTACT NOTICED THERE WAS ABNORMAL ODOR OF FUEL IN THE OIL. THE CHECK ENGINE WARNING LIGH
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Engine problems show up in 63 complaints (19%). None of these reports mention a crash, fire, injury, or death.

issues.sample: APPROXIMATELY RIGHT AFTER I PURCHASED THE CAR, I BEGAN HAVING TROUBLE WITH THE BRAKE. THE BRAKE SYSTEM IS HARDER THAN NORMAL; WHEN I APPLY PRESSURE TO THE BRAKE PEDAL, IT FEELS LIKE IT HAS SEVERAL LAYERS UNTIL I PUSH IT TO THE END. IT FEELS LIKE IT DOESN'T RESPOND RIGHT AWAY. ALS
issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Service brakes account for 55 complaints (17%). 2 mention a crash and 2 mention an injury; no fires or deaths reported.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Electrical system issues appear in 47 complaints (14%). These include 1 fire, 2 crashes, and 4 injuries; no deaths reported.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Fuel and propulsion problems make up 43 complaints (13%). None of these reports mention a crash, fire, injury, or death.
